title: Salty Tender Malaysian Shiitake Fried Rice
description: A savory, comforting Malaysian dish featuring tender shiitake mushrooms and a blend of salty, umami flavors, perfect for a sick day. This Jain-friendly recipe is easy to make and delicious to eat.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced shiitake mushrooms and cook until they release their moisture and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the cooked rice to the skillet or wok, breaking up any clumps with a spatula.
Stir-fry the rice with the mushrooms and onion for about 5 minutes, until everything is well combined and the rice is heated through.
Add the soy sauce, salt, and pepper to the rice mixture and stir-fry for another minute.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Transfer the fried rice to a serving platter and garnish with chopped scallions.
